response.justification: The candidate is a skilled and versatile senior developer, but his profile does not align with the core requirement of this role: being an experienced C++ developer. His only professional C++ experience was a brief, 7-month stint nearly a decade ago. His career since then has focused heavily on web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js) and the D programming language. While he has experience in high-performance systems and some industrial exposure, the fundamental lack of recent and deep C++ expertise, coupled with no mention of the required IEC 62443 security standards, makes him a poor fit.
strengths: Extensive experience as a senior software engineer across diverse domains and technologies.
Demonstrated ability to build high-performance, low-latency applications and distributed systems.
Has relevant, albeit dated, experience in the industrial software domain (National Instruments).
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal, outdated (7 months in 2015), and not a primary skill.
No mention of experience with key security requirements like SDL or IEC 62443 standards.
Primary expertise is in web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js) and Dlang, not C++.
